title: Waiting for Resource Conditions
description:
## Waiting for Resource Conditions with `kubectl wait`

`kubectl wait` blocks until a specified condition is met on a resource — essential in scripts and CI pipelines where you need to ensure resources are ready before proceeding.

Common conditions:
- `--for=condition=Ready` — pod is Ready
- `--for=condition=Available` — deployment has minimum available replicas
- `--for=condition=Complete` — job has completed
- `--for=delete` — resource has been deleted

**Your task:**

1. Create a Deployment named `wait-demo` with image `nginx:alpine` and 3 replicas
2. Use `kubectl wait` to **block until the Deployment is Available** (timeout: 90 seconds)
3. Use `kubectl wait` to block until **all pods** with label `app=wait-demo` are Ready
4. Save the output of `kubectl get deployment wait-demo` to `/tmp/wait-result.txt` after the waits complete

```bash
kubectl wait deployment/wait-demo --for=condition=Available --timeout=90s
kubectl wait pods -l app=wait-demo --for=condition=Ready --timeout=90s
```

hints.title: Create the deployment and wait
hints.body: Chain the create and wait commands together.
hints.command: kubectl create deployment wait-demo --image=nginx:alpine --replicas=3 && kubectl wait deployment/wait-demo --for=condition=Available --timeout=90s

hints.title: Wait for pods by label
hints.body: Use -l to select by label and wait for all selected pods to be Ready.

hints.title: Save deployment status
hints.body: Redirect kubectl get output to the required file.

commands.command: kubectl get deployment wait-demo -o jsonpath='{.status.readyReplicas}' 2>/dev/null | grep -v '^$' || echo 0

commands.description: File /tmp/wait-result.txt exists and contains deployment info
